summary: Ensures CI‑OS processes meet governance, compliance, and risk‑management standards through structured audits, risk assessments, and continuous oversight.
responsibilities: Plan, schedule, and execute internal audits across CI‑OS processes, systems, and roles
Identify compliance gaps, process deviations, and systemic risks
Collaborate with Quality Manager and Safety & Compliance Officer on corrective actions
Maintain the enterprise risk register and ensure risks are properly classified and mitigated
Conduct risk assessments for new processes, digital systems, and improvement initiatives
Verify that corrective and preventive actions (CAPA) are implemented and effective
Ensure audit trails, documentation, and governance artifacts are complete and accurate
Support regulatory audits and external compliance reviews
Monitor risk indicators and escalate emerging threats to leadership
Provide guidance on risk‑based decision‑making and governance best practices
authorities: Access all CI‑OS records, logs, and documentation
Classify risks and assign severity levels
Mandate corrective actions for high‑risk findings
Escalate critical risks to the Executive Sponsor
decisionRights: Definition of audit criteria and risk thresholds
Escalation of governance or compliance failures
kaizenCapabilities: Identification of governance‑related waste and inefficiencies
Support for improvement teams in risk‑aware decision‑making
cqiCapabilities: Integration of risk metrics into CQI dashboards
Root‑cause analysis for systemic governance issues

constraints: All findings must be evidence‑based and documented
Risk classifications must follow governance standards
